preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context for SaaS trial users, immediately acknowledging their existing experience with the product and framing the page around why upgrading unlocks their full potential. Sets the Validator archetype at ease by being transparent about what this page is about.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: With trial users already somewhat familiar, this beat articulates the specific capabilities and outcomes they gain by converting to paid — bridging the gap between what they've sampled and the full product promise. Positioned early to give the Validator substantive content to evaluate.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators need proof from trusted sources before committing. Social proof, customer logos, and authority signals validate that other professionals and companies have made the same transition from trial to paid and found it worthwhile. Placed after Value so proof directly reinforces the claims just made.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Deepens the Validator's confidence with concrete metrics, case study data, and quantified outcomes. This satisfies the archetype's need for hard proof and moves beyond logos into verifiable results — critical for a user weighing whether their trial experience will scale into lasting ROI.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Awareness-stage guidance calls for a soft conversion. Rather than aggressive pricing or plan selection, this beat offers a gentle CTA — such as 'Unlock your full account' or 'Continue where you left off' — respecting that trial users may still be early in their evaluation while providing a clear, low-friction path to paid.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Provides secondary paths for Validators who aren't ready to convert yet — links to documentation, a comparison guide, a 'talk to sales' option, or an extended trial request. Ensures no visitor hits a dead end and captures intent even from those who need more time to validate their decision.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: The semantic intelligence layer for code-first SaaS teams. Components that resolve to the right behavior, visual weight, and copy register — for every archetype, funnel stage, and context. No manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription, no lock-in.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires social-proof variants. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, establishing trust while maintaining conversion intent for awareness stage.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: The semantic intelligence layer for code-first SaaS teams. Components that resolve to the right behavior, visual weight, and copy register — for every archetype, funnel stage, and context. No manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription, no lock-in.

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This combination establishes credibility through both narrative proof and recognizable brands, critical for converting trial users in awareness stage.

slots.headline: Used by product teams who ship in code, not Figma handoff cycles.
slots.quote: We cut variant QA from three rounds to zero. The resolution engine makes the right call — we just configure it once and it holds across every page.
slots.attribution: Senior Product Engineer, B2B SaaS — 2024 customer survey

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages. Essential for trial-to-paid conversion goal, preventing drop-off at page close.
